name: Care Home Documentation & CQC (UK)
description: Reference policy aligned with the CQC Fundamental Standards and the single assessment framework.

data.text: Care home documentation — CQC Fundamental Standards
Each resident has a person-centred care plan covering safe, effective, caring, responsive, and well-led domains; reviewed at admission and on change of need.
Consent is recorded; mental-capacity assessments and DoLS authorisations are documented when capacity is in doubt.
Statutory notifications to the CQC are documented for relevant incidents; a record is kept of decision and rationale.
Medication administration is recorded on a MAR; controlled drugs follow stricter recording requirements with witness sign-off.
Incidents (falls, medication, safeguarding, behaviour) are documented at the time and reviewed by the manager.
Documentation supporting CQC inspections — care plans, incident logs, audit cycle, complaints, training records — is kept current.

clauses.title: Person-centred care plan
clauses.body: Each resident must have a person-centred care plan covering safe, effective, caring, responsive, and well-led domains; reviewed at admission and on change of need.

clauses.title: Consent + Mental Capacity Act
clauses.body: Consent must be recorded; mental-capacity assessments and DoLS authorisations documented when capacity is in doubt.

clauses.title: CQC notifications recorded
clauses.body: Statutory notifications to the CQC must be documented for relevant incidents; record of decision and rationale kept.

clauses.title: MAR + controlled-drug witness
clauses.body: Medication administration must be recorded on a MAR; controlled drugs follow stricter recording requirements with witness sign-off.

clauses.title: Incident reporting
clauses.body: Incidents must be documented at the time and reviewed by the manager.

clauses.title: Inspection-ready documentation
clauses.body: Documentation supporting CQC inspections must be kept current — care plans, incident logs, audit cycle, complaints, training records.
